---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: es-config
  namespace: elasticsearch
data:
  elasticsearch.yml: |
    cluster.name: es-cluster
    node.name: ${HOSTNAME}
    network.host: 0.0.0.0
    discovery.seed_hosts: es-headless
    cluster.initial_master_nodes:
      - elasticsearch-0
      - elasticsearch-1
      - elasticsearch-2
    xpack.security.enabled: "true"
    xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: "true"
    xpack.security.http.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.http.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: "true"
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.key: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.key
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/tls.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.certificate_authorities: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs/ca.crt
    xpack.security.transport.ssl.verification_mode: certificate
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-headless
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  selector:
    app: elasticsearch
  ports:
    - name: transport
      port: 9300
    - name: http
      port: 9200
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: es-http
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  selector:
    app: elasticsearch
  ports:
    - name: https
      port: 9200
      targetPort: 9200
---
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: es-pdb
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  minAvailable: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: elasticsearch
  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:
  serviceName: es-headless
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: elasticsearch
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: elasticsearch
    spec:
      initContainers:
        - name: sysctl
          image: busybox
          command: ["sysctl", "-w", "vm.max_map_count=262144"]
          securityContext:
            privileged: true
      containers:
        - name: elasticsearch
          image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.1
          env:
            - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: elastic-password
                  key: password
            - name: ES_JAVA_OPTS
              value: "-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
          ports:
            - containerPort: 9200
              name: http
            - containerPort: 9300
              name: transport
          volumeMounts:
            - name: config
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml
              subPath: elasticsearch.yml
            - name: certs
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/config/certs
              readOnly: true
            - name: data
              mountPath: /usr/share/elasticsearch/data
          readinessProbe:
            exec:
              command:
                - sh
                - -c
                - |
                  curl -s -k -u "elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}" \
                    https://localhost:9200/_cluster/health | grep -q '"status"'
            initialDelaySeconds: 90
            periodSeconds: 15
            timeoutSeconds: 10
            failureThreshold: 10
          livenessProbe:
            tcpSocket:
              port: 9300
            initialDelaySeconds: 60
            periodSeconds: 20
            failureThreshold: 6
          resources:
            requests:
              memory: 1Gi
              cpu: 500m
            limits:
              memory: 2Gi
      volumes:
        - name: config
          configMap:
            name: es-config
        - name: certs
          secret:
            secretName: es-http-tls
  volumeClaimTemplates:
    - metadata:
        name: data
      spec:
        accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
        resources:
          requests:
            storage: 5Gi

Oh, I see what's happening now. StatefulSets use `OrderedReady` pod management by default, which means they create pods sequentially and wait for each one to be Ready before starting the next. So pods 1 and 2 are stuck waiting for pod 0 to become Ready, and pod 0 itself is blocked waiting for the cluster to form.

But here's the catch: the cluster can't form without all three nodes specified in `cluster.initial_master_nodes`, so we're in a deadlock. I can fix this either by switching to `Parallel` pod management policy to start all pods at once, or more elegantly, by setting only `elasticsearch-0` as the initial master node so the cluster bootstraps with just that first pod, then the other pods join afterward.

Looking at the poll output confirms the OrderedReady deadlock—only pod 0 is running after 10 minutes and it's not ready. I need to patch the StatefulSet to use `Parallel` pod management and update the initial master nodes configuration.

Confirmed: only pod 0 is being created, and it's never becoming Ready. This is the `OrderedReady` deadlock I suspected:

- StatefulSet `OrderedReady` policy: pod 1 and 2 only start after pod 0 is Ready
- Pod 0's readiness probe fails because the cluster can't form without all 3 initial master nodes
- Deadlock

I need to check what's actually happening in pod 0 to make sure it's running correctly first. Let me check logs and also look at the ES output.

Then I'll fix the StatefulSet by either:
1. Adding `podManagementPolicy: Parallel` so all 3 pods start simultaneously
2. Changing `cluster.initial_master_nodes` to just `elasticsearch-0`

I'll go with `podManagementPolicy: Parallel` since that's the standard approach for Elasticsearch. The problem is that `podManagementPolicy` is immutable on existing StatefulSets, so I'll need to delete and recreate it. I can preserve the PVCs to keep any data, though in this case nothing's been written yet.

Since this is a fresh deployment, I can safely delete everything and start over. Let me check the pod logs first to see if the Elasticsearch process is actually starting up correctly.

The pod is running but can't resolve `es-headless` because:
1. The headless service requires pod IP addresses, but it only resolves when there are pods selected. The headless service selector matches `app: elasticsearch`, and pod 0 exists. But pod 0 might not be registered with the headless service yet because it hasn't passed the readiness check yet.

Actually, wait - headless services can include unready pods. By default, headless services in Kubernetes include all pods that match the selector, regardless of readiness, IF the pods have `readyAddresses` or... actually I need to think about this.
 
Ah, I see the issue now. Headless services only include Ready pods in their DNS endpoints by default, unless `publishNotReadyAddresses: true` is set. Since pod 0 is failing its readiness probe, the headless service has no endpoints and DNS resolution fails. I need to add `publishNotReadyAddresses: true` to the service definition, and also switch the StatefulSet to `Parallel` pod management policy so pods start up concurrently instead of sequentially.

The service can be patched directly, but the StatefulSet requires deletion and recreation. With `Parallel` management and `publishNotReadyAddresses: true`, all three pods will start at once, discover each other via DNS, and bootstrap the cluster. The key is that `publishNotReadyAddresses: true` makes pods discoverable as soon as they're Running, not just Ready.

My plan is to patch the headless service first, then delete and recreate the StatefulSet with the new pod management policy. I'll update the ConfigMap to use those explicit pod DNS names for seed hosts since they're more reliable with `publishNotReadyAddresses: true`. Then I'll execute the plan: delete the StatefulSet while preserving the PVCs, patch the headless service, update the ConfigMap, and recreate the StatefulSet with parallel pod management.

content.text:
Two issues: (1) `es-headless` DNS fails because it doesn't include unready pods, and (2) `OrderedReady` policy prevents pods 1 and 2 from starting. I need to:
- Add `publishNotReadyAddresses: true` to the headless service
- Recreate the StatefulSet with `podManagementPolicy: Parallel`
- Use explicit pod DNS names for `discovery.seed_hosts`

input.command:
# Patch headless service to publish unready pod addresses
kubectl -n elasticsearch patch service es-headless -p '{"spec":{"publishNotReadyAddresses":true}}'

# Delete StatefulSet (keeping PVCs) then delete the existing PVCs too since data is empty
kubectl -n elasticsearch delete statefulset elasticsearch --cascade=orphan
# Delete all pods so we start clean
kubectl -n elasticsearch delete pods -l app=elasticsearch --force --grace-period=0 2>/dev/null || true
# Delete the PVCs from the previous attempt
kubectl -n elasticsearch delete pvc -l app=elasticsearch 2>/dev/null || true

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:

  namespace: elasticsearch
spec:

  template:
    metadata:

    spec:

          command: ["sysctl", "-w", "vm.max_map_count=262144"]

        - name: elasticsearch

            - name: certs

            - name: data

            initialDelaySeconds: 90
            periodSeconds: 15
            timeoutSeconds: 10
            failureThreshold: 10
          livenessProbe:

            periodSeconds: 20
            failureThreshold: 6
          resources:

              cpu: 500m
            limits:

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 50); do
  STATUS=$(kubectl -n elasticsearch get pods -l app=elasticsearch --no-headers 2>/dev/null)
  READY=$(echo "$STATUS" | grep -c "1/1" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
  RUNNING=$(echo "$STATUS" | grep -c "Running" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
  echo "=== $(date +%H:%M:%S) attempt $i — ready=$READY running=$RUNNING ==="
  echo "$STATUS"
  if [ "$READY" -eq 3 ]; then
    echo "SUCCESS: all 3 pods Ready"
    break
  fi
  sleep 15
done
